question_text: Suppose that X1, X2, . . . , Xn are independent and identically distributed random variables each having probability density function f(·) and median θ. We want to test H0 : θ = θ0

options.C: onsider a test that rejects H0 if S > c for some c depending on the size of the test, where S is the cardinality of the set {i : Xi > θ0, 1 ≤i ≤n}. Then which one of the following statements is true?
options.B: Under H1, the distribution of S does not depend on f(·)
options.D: The power function does not depend on θ

solution:
1) Understanding the power function:
The power function of a test is defined as the probability of rejecting H0 when the true
parameter is θ, i.e., β(θ) = P(Rejecting H0 | θ). The power function depends on how the test
behaves under different values of θ.
2) Analysis:
- The test uses the statistic S, the number of Xi greater than θ0. Under H0, this statistic
follows a binomial distribution with parameters n and the probability P(Xi > θ0). - The
distribution of S is affected by the value of θ, and so is the power of the test. Hence, the
power function depends on θ.
Thus, the correct answer is (C).
Quick Tip
- The power function of a hypothesis test depends on how the test behaves for different
values of the parameter θ under consideration.
- A test’s power increases as the distance between the null hypothesis and alternative
hypothesis increases.
